source: RELAB Spectral Library, PDS4 bundle at the PDS Geosciences Node
selection: specimen_type == 'Returned Asteroid Sample'. Returned Lunar Samples (1,510) deliberately excluded: the Moon is not an asteroid (out of scope for an asteroid space-weathering reference).
sample_note: All returned-asteroid spectra in this bundle are Ryugu sample C0002 (Hayabusa2 / JAXA), measured at the Brown University RELAB facility (owner/requestor Ralph E. Milliken). Collection location per label e.g. 'Asteroid Ryugu SCI crater' (artificial-impact subsurface material). The PDS4 labels carry no per-sample paper DOI; sample origin traces to the Hayabusa2 return (Yada et al. 2022, Nat. Astron. 6, 214; Pilorget et al. 2022).
citation: Pieters, C.M., Hiroi, T. (2004), RELAB (Reflectance Experiment Laboratory): A NASA Multiuser Spectroscopy Facility, LPSC XXXV, #1720; Milliken, R. et al., RELAB Spectral Library Bundle urn:nasa:pds:relab V3.0, PDS Geosciences Node; PDS Spectral Library: doi:10.3847/PSJ/ad5af7; retrieved 2026-07-07
license_terms: NASA PDS open data (publicly released, no access restrictions). RELAB is a NASA-supported multiuser facility; publications using these data customarily acknowledge the RELAB facility at Brown University and cite Pieters & Hiroi (2004) and the PDS Spectral Library.
